gist.headline: Cleaning up the name tags
gist.blurb: We've added a quick scrub to chat message names before they hit the API. No more mysterious 500 errors when your metadata gets a little too creative for Grok.
gist.summary: Added sanitization for optional message names in chat payloads to enforce upstream regex contracts and prevent provider-side 500 errors.
gist.impact: API users will experience fewer unexpected 500 errors when sending chat requests with malformed metadata names, particularly when using Grok models.
gist.visual_concept: The Nomnom creature eating invalid/messy name tags before they reach the server, representing the sanitization of message names. Polly the bee oversees the process.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating messy, scribbled name tags off a conveyor belt, while the bee mascot wearing a tiny inspector hat watches with a clipboard. A retro terminal nearby glows lime green (#ecf874) showing clean code. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like A Short Hike.
